title: Checkpoint and resume capability
description:
As a score optimizer
I want to resume interrupted optimizations from checkpoints
So that long-running optimizations can survive interruptions

## Acceptance Criteria

- Procedure state persists to database after each iteration
- Can resume from any checkpoint
- Completed iterations are not re-executed
- State includes baseline metrics, iteration logs, and RCA data

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Checkpoint and Resume for Long-Running Optimizations
  As a score optimizer
  I want to resume interrupted optimization runs
  So that I don't lose progress from completed iterations

Scenario: Resume after graceful stop
  Given an optimization is running for score "empathy"
  And iteration 1 has completed successfully
  And iteration 2 is about to start
  When I stop the procedure with Ctrl+C
  And I run: plexus procedure resume <procedure-id>
  Then the procedure resumes from iteration 2
  And iteration 1 is not re-executed
  And the baseline evaluation is reused
  And all previous iteration data is preserved

Scenario: Resume after system crash
  Given an optimization is running
  And 2 iterations have completed
  And the system crashes during iteration 3
  When the system restarts
  And I run: plexus procedure resume <procedure-id>
  Then the procedure resumes from the point of failure
  And iterations 1-2 results are preserved
  And the baseline is still referenced correctly

Scenario: Checkpoint storage in database
  Given an optimization with completed iterations
  When I query the Procedure record
  Then the metadata field contains:
    | Field | Present |
    | baseline_evaluation_id | Yes |
    | baseline_metrics | Yes |
    | iterations | Yes |
    | current_iteration | Yes |
    | last_rca | Yes |
  And each iteration log includes:
    | Field | Present |
    | iteration | Yes |
    | hypothesis | Yes |
    | score_version_id | Yes |
    | evaluation_id | Yes |
    | metrics | Yes |
    | deltas | Yes |
```
